title: agnos.json
description: Single source of truth for an agnos project: which agents are active, how rules are composed, what skills/MCP servers/hooks are configured, and how docs are managed.

$schema.description: URL of this JSON schema. Recommended so editors can validate and autocomplete agnos.json.

schemaVersion.description: agnos.json schema version. Must be 1; older/absent values are rejected — re-run `agnos --init`.

rules.description: Rules domain. Maps each canonical rules file to the injectable fragment files whose titled sections are injected into it.

files.description: Map of canonical rules file path → array of injectable fragment file paths.

skills.description: Skills domain configuration: where canonical content lives and which skills to install.

route.description: Canonical skills directory, project-relative. Defaults to ".agnos/skills". Agents link their own skills dir to this path.

sources.description: Map of local skill name → composite source ref. Grammar: "github:<owner>/<repo>/<in-repo-path>" (or gitlab:/bitbucket:), or "file:<path-to-skill-dir>".

mcp.description: MCP server declarations, rendered into per-agent MCP config (.mcp.json for Claude Code, .codex/config.toml for Codex).

name.description: Local name for this MCP server.

source.description: Optional source ref for the server package.

env.description: Environment variables. Values may contain ${VAR} references resolved at render time.

transport.description: Transport protocol the agent uses to talk to this server.

hooks.description: Hooks registry: a flat array of command hooks. Agents render each entry into their native format (regrouping by event/matcher), mapping `message` to native status text and skipping unsupported events.

matcher.description: Optional filter for when the hook fires (meaning depends on the event).

type.description: Handler type. Only "command" is supported in v0.1.

message.description: User-facing status text shown while the hook runs.

docs.description: Docs domain configuration. Watches `root`, compiles an index from the docs' frontmatter metadata.

metadata.description: Map of frontmatter key → natural-language description, merged onto the opinionated defaults (title/description/read_when/agent_cant).
